articles.title: Finland Becomes First NATO Country to Allow Nuclear Weapons on Its Soil
articles.summary: Finland's parliament made history this week by voting to end a long-standing ban on nuclear weapons. The new law allows nuclear weapons to be brought into, stored, and transported across Finnish territory when needed for military defence. Finland joined NATO in 2023, and its government says this change is needed so the country can cooperate fully with other NATO members. The decision does not mean Finland will own nuclear weapons — it simply means allies could use Finnish land. Not everyone agreed; some lawmakers warned this could make Finland a target and increase tensions with Russia, which shares a long border with the country.

lv1: What did Finland's parliament vote to change this week? (芬蘭國會今週投票決定改變什麼？)
Which military alliance does Finland belong to? (芬蘭屬於哪個軍事聯盟？)
lv2: Why do you think Finland decided to allow nuclear weapons on its territory now, and how might this affect its relationship with Russia? (你認為芬蘭為什麼現在決定允許核武器進入其領土，這可能如何影響它與俄羅斯的關係？)
What is the difference between a country owning nuclear weapons and allowing allies to store them there, and why does this distinction matter? (一個國家自己擁有核武器，與允許盟友在當地儲存核武器，有什麼分別？為什麼這個區別很重要？)
lv3: Does allowing nuclear weapons on a country's soil make that country safer or more dangerous? Consider arguments from both sides. (允許核武器進入一個國家的領土，究竟令該國更安全還是更危險？請從正反兩方面討論。)
Should smaller countries near powerful neighbours be allowed to make their own decisions about nuclear weapons, even if it risks upsetting regional peace? (鄰近強國的小國，即使這樣做可能破壞地區和平，是否應有權自行決定核武政策？)

articles.title: China Cuts Rare Earth Exports to Japan by Over 80 Percent
articles.summary: New trade data from early 2026 shows that China sharply reduced the amount of rare earth minerals it sends to Japan, with exports falling by more than eighty percent compared to the same period last year. Rare earth minerals are essential ingredients for making electric vehicles, smartphones, and defence equipment. Experts believe the cuts are linked to worsening relations between the two countries, with China using its control of these resources as strategic leverage. Japan depends heavily on these materials for its technology sector, and the sudden drop has prompted urgent efforts to find alternative suppliers in other parts of the world.

lv1: By roughly how much did China reduce its rare earth exports to Japan? (中國大約減少了多少對日本的稀土出口？)
Can you name one product that uses rare earth minerals? (你能說出一種使用稀土礦物的產品嗎？)
lv2: Why might a country choose to cut exports of an important resource to another country instead of using other forms of pressure? (為什麼一個國家可能選擇削減向另一個國家出口重要資源，而不是採用其他施壓方式？)
How could Japan protect itself from depending too much on one country for critical materials in the future? (日本未來如何保護自己，避免過度依賴單一國家供應關鍵物資？)
lv3: Is it fair or ethical for a country to use its natural resources as a political weapon against another nation? What international rules, if any, should apply? (一個國家以天然資源作為對付另一個國家的政治武器，是否公平或合乎道德？應否有國際規則加以規管？)

How does control over critical minerals like rare earths shape the balance of power between nations in the modern world, and what does this mean for smaller economies? (對稀土等關鍵礦物的控制，如何影響現代國家之間的權力平衡，這對較小的經濟體意味著什麼？)

articles.title: 香港首個五年規劃展開公眾諮詢　市民共同參與繪製城市藍圖
articles.summary: 香港特區政府正式啟動首個「五年規劃」的公眾諮詢工作，邀請各界市民就香港未來五年的發展方向提出意見。這是香港歷史上首次制定與國家「十五五規劃」相配合的本地發展計劃，涵蓋經濟、教育、科技及民生等多個範疇。政府希望透過廣泛諮詢，集思廣益，讓不同背景的市民都能參與城市未來的規劃過程，共同為香港的長遠發展出謀獻策。

lv1: 香港政府現時正就什麼計劃進行公眾諮詢？
這是香港第幾次制定五年規劃？
lv2: 為什麼政府需要聽取市民意見，才能制定城市的發展方向？
香港的「五年規劃」與國家的「十五五規劃」有什麼關係，為什麼要互相配合？
lv3: 公眾諮詢能否真正反映所有市民的想法？哪些群體的聲音可能較難被聽到，政府應如何改善諮詢方式？
香港在制定五年規劃時，應該優先發展哪些範疇？試從年輕人、長者及商界人士等不同角度分析他們各自的需要。

articles.title: 梵蒂岡發表首份AI通諭　教宗警告科技壟斷威脅人類尊嚴
articles.summary: 梵蒂岡近日發布教宗良十四世就任後的首份通諭，主題聚焦人工智能（AI）對人類社會的深遠影響。這份長達四萬餘字的文件指出，AI技術急速發展帶來了科技壟斷危機，部分大型科技企業的影響力不斷膨脹，可能凌駕於各國政府及個人之上。教宗呼籲全球社會確保AI發展以尊重人類尊嚴為核心，並特別反對將AI應用於軍事用途，強調科技應服務於人，而非支配人類。

lv1: 這份通諭的主要話題是什麼？
是哪位教宗發表了這份關於人工智能的通諭？
lv2: 為什麼教宗認為人工智能的快速發展可能對人類尊嚴構成威脅？
「科技壟斷」是什麼意思？如果少數科技公司掌控了大部分AI技術，普通人的生活會受到什麼影響？
lv3: 人工智能的發展應該受到什麼規則約束？監管責任應由政府、科技公司還是國際機構承擔？各方各有什麼優缺點？
將AI應用於軍事用途（例如自動化武器系統）在道德上是否可以接受？請從不同持份者的角度加以討論。
